# google_migration_center_import_job

ImportJob represents a batch data import task that processes uploaded data files and populates Migration Center assets.



<div class = "oics-button" style="float: right; margin: 0 0 -15px">
  <a href="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloudshell/open?cloudshell_git_repo=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fterraform-google-modules%2Fdocs-examples.git&cloudshell_image=gcr.io%2Fcloudshell-images%2Fcloudshell%3Alatest&cloudshell_print=.%2Fmotd&cloudshell_tutorial=.%2Ftutorial.md&cloudshell_working_dir=migration_center_import_job_basic&open_in_editor=main.tf" target="_blank">
    <img alt="Open in Cloud Shell" src="//gstatic.com/cloudssh/images/open-btn.svg" style="max-height: 44px; margin: 32px auto; max-width: 100%;">
  </a>
</div>
## Example Usage - Migration Center Import Job Basic


```hcl
resource "google_migration_center_source" "default" {
  location  = "us-central1"
  source_id = "source-test"
  type      = "SOURCE_TYPE_CUSTOM"
}

resource "google_migration_center_import_job" "default" {
  location      = "us-central1"
  import_job_id = "import-job-test"
  asset_source  = google_migration_center_source.default.id
  display_name  = "Terraform integration test display"
  labels = {
    my_key     = "value"
    second_key = "second_value"
  }
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `asset_source` -
  (Required)
  Reference to a source.

* `location` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

* `import_job_id` -
  (Required)
  ID of the import job.


* `display_name` -
  (Optional)
  User-friendly display name. Maximum length is 256 characters.

* `labels` -
  (Optional)
  Labels as key value pairs.
  **Note**: This field is non-authoritative, and will only manage the labels present in your configuration.
  Please refer to the field `effective_labels` for all of the labels present on the resource.

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

* `deletion_policy` - (Optional) Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the resource. Defaults to DELETE.
	When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the resource,
	the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
	When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
	management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
	When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.


## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/importJobs/{{import_job_id}}`

* `complete_time` -
  The timestamp when the import job was completed.

* `create_time` -
  The timestamp when the import job was created.

* `execution_report` -
  A resource that reports result of the import job execution.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_execution_report).

* `name` -
  The full name of the import job.

* `state` -
  The state of the import job.
  Possible values:
  IMPORT_JOB_STATE_PENDING
  IMPORT_JOB_STATE_RUNNING
  IMPORT_JOB_STATE_COMPLETED
  IMPORT_JOB_STATE_FAILED
  IMPORT_JOB_STATE_VALIDATING
  IMPORT_JOB_STATE_FAILED_VALIDATION
  IMPORT_JOB_STATE_READY

* `update_time` -
  The timestamp when the import job was last updated.

* `validation_report` -
  A resource that aggregates errors across import job files.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_validation_report).

* `terraform_labels` -
  The combination of labels configured directly on the resource
   and default labels configured on the provider.

* `effective_labels` -
  All of labels (key/value pairs) present on the resource in GCP, including the labels configured through Terraform, other clients and services.


<a name="nested_execution_report"></a>The `execution_report` block contains:

* `execution_errors` -
  (Output)
  A resource that aggregates errors across import job files.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_execution_report_execution_errors).

* `frames_reported` -
  (Output)
  Total number of asset frames reported for the import job.

* `total_rows_count` -
  (Output)
  Total number of rows in the import job.


<a name="nested_execution_report_execution_errors"></a>The `execution_errors` block contains:

* `file_validations` -
  (Output)
  List of errors found in files.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations).

* `job_errors` -
  (Output)
  List of job level errors.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_execution_report_execution_errors_job_errors).


<a name="nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations"></a>The `file_validations` block contains:

* `file_errors` -
  (Output)
  List of file level errors.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_file_errors).

* `file_name` -
  (Output)
  The name of the file.

* `partial_report` -
  (Output)
  Flag indicating that processing was aborted due to maximum number of
  errors.

* `row_errors` -
  (Output)
  Partial list of rows that encountered validation error.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors).


<a name="nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_file_errors"></a>The `file_errors` block contains:

* `error_details` -
  (Output)
  The error information.

* `severity` -
  (Output)
  The severity of the error.
  Possible values:
  ERROR
  WARNING
  INFO

<a name="nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors"></a>The `row_errors` block contains:

* `archive_error` -
  (Output)
  Error details for an archive file.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors_archive_error).

* `asset_title` -
  (Output)
  The asset title.

* `csv_error` -
  (Output)
  Error details for a CSV file.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors_csv_error).

* `errors` -
  (Output)
  The list of errors detected in the row.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors_errors).

* `row_number` -
  (Output)
  The row number where the error was detected.

* `vm_name` -
  (Output)
  The name of the VM in the row.

* `vm_uuid` -
  (Output)
  The VM UUID.

* `xlsx_error` -
  (Output)
  Error details for an XLSX file.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors_xlsx_error).


<a name="nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors_archive_error"></a>The `archive_error` block contains:

* `csv_error` -
  (Output)
  Error details for a CSV file.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors_archive_error_csv_error).

* `file_path` -
  (Output)
  The file path inside the archive where the error was detected.


<a name="nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors_archive_error_csv_error"></a>The `csv_error` block contains:

* `row_number` -
  (Output)
  The row number where the error was detected.

<a name="nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors_csv_error"></a>The `csv_error` block contains:

* `row_number` -
  (Output)
  The row number where the error was detected.

<a name="nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors_errors"></a>The `errors` block contains:

* `error_details` -
  (Output)
  The error information.

* `severity` -
  (Output)
  The severity of the error.
  Possible values:
  ERROR
  WARNING
  INFO

<a name="nested_execution_report_execution_errors_file_validations_row_errors_xlsx_error"></a>The `xlsx_error` block contains:

* `row_number` -
  (Output)
  The row number where the error was detected.

  (Output)
  The name of the sheet where the error was detected.

## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `update` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 20 minutes.

## Import


ImportJob can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/importJobs/{{import_job_id}}`
* `{{project}}/{{location}}/{{import_job_id}}`
* `{{location}}/{{import_job_id}}`

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, use an [`identity` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/identities) to import ImportJob using identity values. For example:

```tf
import {
  identity = {
    location = "<-required value->"
    importJobId = "<-required value->"
    project = "<-optional value->"
  }
  to = google_migration_center_import_job.default
}
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import ImportJob using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/importJobs/{{import_job_id}}"
  to = google_migration_center_import_job.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), ImportJob can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_migration_center_import_job.default projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/importJobs/{{import_job_id}}
$ terraform import google_migration_center_import_job.default {{project}}/{{location}}/{{import_job_id}}
$ terraform import google_migration_center_import_job.default {{location}}/{{import_job_id}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
